sections.paragraph:
an unused root; Arab. سَالَ Med. Waw, to be loose, pendulous (specially, the belly); compare ذال to drag the train of one’s robe (used of a woman). Hence-

שול

m.-

(1) the train, flowing skirt of a robe, i.q. שֹׁבֶלּ Isa 6:1. גִּלָּה שׁוּלִים to uncover the skirt, i.e. to expose to the greatest shame, Jer 13:22, 26 Jer 13:26 Nah 3:5.

(2) hem of a garment, Exo 28:33, 34 Exo 28:34.
